title: crotalaria eremaea subsp. eremaea
intro: Crotalaria eremaea subsp. eremaea, also known as the desert rattle, is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia. This plant contains bioactive compounds with medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can be used to treat various health issues. The desert rattle is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Traditionally, it is prepared for herbal use by drying the leaves and flowers, and can be consumed as a tea or used in topical applications.
